summary: This document specifies compression of `uint256` to smaller data structures like `uint64`, `uint96`, `uint128`, for optimizing costs for storage. The smaller data structure (represented as `cintx`) is divided into two parts, in the first one we store `significant` bits and in the other number of left `shift`s needed on the significant bits to decompress. This document also includes two specifications for decompression due to the nature of compression being lossy, i.e. it causes underflow.
title: Compressed Integers
